summary: Joel Spolsky puts out a call for volunteers to translate his blog 'Joel on Software' into as many languages as possible. He emphasizes flexibility, allowing contributors to translate as much or as little as they want in any language. He also seeks volunteer copy editors to review incoming translations.
key_points: Joel seeks to expand his blog's reach through community-driven translations
The volunteer model is flexible — contributors choose their own scope and language
Copy editors are needed alongside translators to maintain quality
money_quotes: It would be nice to get Joel on Software translated to as many languages as possible.
You can do as little or as much as you want to any language you want.
key_insight: This is a brief administrative call for translation volunteers, not a substantive blog post with technical or strategic content.
